pi_items.source: Clinical guideline: Hypotension is defined as systolic BP <90 mmHg per Mayo Clinic, Cleveland Clinic, NIH, AHA. Task description explicitly states 'below 90 mmHg' as the critical threshold.

Task description specifies 'above 500 mL/hr' as bolus rate. Clinical practice supports 500+ mL/hr as emergency vasopressor bolus rate per Stanford anesthesia resources and emergency medicine protocols.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic medical simulation task using OpenICE, an open-source platform developed by the MD PnP Program at Massachusetts General Hospital. The task simulates a hypotension emergency response drill. The target blood pressure threshold of 90 mmHg systolic is clinically accurate per authoritative medical sources (Mayo Clinic, AHA, NIH, Cleveland Clinic), as hypotension is defined as systolic BP below 90 mmHg. The target infusion rate of 500 mL/hr minimum is consistent with emergency bolus vasopressor administration protocols. All metadata claims about file paths and target parameters are verified against the task description and medical clinical guidelines.
